question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: maxBlockItems field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that pending.clear() and pendingCount = 0 only happen in onEndOfBlock(), meaning that if an attacker causes the stream to never send an END_OF_BLOCK message, the pendingCount keeps growing across multiple BlockItemSet messages until it exceeds maxBlockItems, triggering a BlockStreamException that deactivates the node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: responseTimeout field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the single-threaded executor in BlockNodeSubscriber used for draining gRPC buffers, by causing many concurrent stream failures (each triggering drainGrpcBuffer), flooding the executor queue with drain tasks that each block for up to responseTimeout milliseconds, causing memory exhaustion and further degrading the system's ability to recover from the partition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the gRPC channel reuse in BlockNode (channel is created once in the constructor and reused) by causing a single oversized response that exceeds maxStreamResponseSize, which may put the gRPC channel into an error state that persists across subsequent stream attempts, causing all future streams on that channel to fail and permanently deactivating the node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the errors.set(0) reset in BlockNode.onError() (which resets the counter after deactivation) combined with the errors.set(0) in streamBlocks() (which resets on success), by alternating between causing exactly maxSubscribeAttempts-1 failures followed by one success, keeping the error counter perpetually below the deactivation threshold while still causing significant disruption to block streaming?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: readmitDelay field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readmitDelay to amplify the impact of a brief network disruption, where a 1-second attack causing 3 failures results in a 60-second (default readmitDelay) outage per node, achieving a 60x amplification factor that turns a minor network blip into a sustained partition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: maxBlockItems field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BlockAssembler's lack of a timeout on how long a block can remain in the pending state, combined with maxBlockItems, by slowly trickling block items just below the maxBlockItems threshold to keep the assembler in a perpetually pending state, preventing any block from being completed and delivered to the verifier?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: responseTimeout field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that responseTimeout is applied per-read in grpcCall.read(assembler.timeout(), TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS), by causing the first read to succeed (resetting the stopwatch) but subsequent reads to time out, creating a pattern where the assembler starts accumulating block items but never completes a block, while each timeout triggers onError() and contributes to node deactivation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxStreamResponseSize controls the gRPC maxInboundMessageSize at the channel level (not per-call), meaning that a single large message that triggers the limit may cause the entire ManagedChannel to enter a degraded state, affecting all subsequent calls including server status checks, making the node appear partitioned even after the large message is no longer being sent?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the synchronized getBlockNodes() method in BlockNodeSubscriber by causing rapid node property changes (via RegisteredNodeChangedEvent) that trigger node recreation, combined with stream failures, to create a race condition where nodes are closed and recreated while errors are being counted, potentially resetting error counters and preventing proper deactivation during a real partition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: readmitDelay field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readmitDelay by observing that when a node is deactivated, readmitTime is set to Instant.now().plus(readmitDelay), and by causing the system to be under high load during the readmit window, the tryReadmit(false) check may not be called promptly, extending the effective partition duration beyond the configured readmitDelay?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: maxBlockItems field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the memory allocation in BlockAssembler by sending maxBlockItems (800,000) block items each containing large payloads, causing the pending list to consume excessive heap memory before the limit check triggers BlockStreamException, potentially causing an OutOfMemoryError that crashes the importer process rather than gracefully deactivating the node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: responseTimeout field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between responseTimeout and the BlockAssembler.timeout() method, where timeout() returns timeout.toMillis() - stopwatch.elapsed(TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS), and if the elapsed time exceeds timeout.toMillis(), the return value becomes negative, causing grpcCall.read() to be called with a negative timeout which may behave as a zero-timeout and immediately return null, terminating the stream without triggering onError()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DataSize.ofMegabytes(36) default for maxStreamResponseSize by observing that legitimate block responses near this size limit are common, and by injecting network congestion that causes TCP fragmentation and reassembly delays, making the gRPC layer believe the message is incomplete and timing out, triggering onError() for legitimate large blocks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the error counter in BlockNode is per-node (AtomicInteger errors) and not shared across nodes, by targeting each node individually with exactly maxSubscribeAttempts failures in sequence, systematically deactivating all nodes one by one to create a complete partition without triggering any cross-node rate limiting?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: readmitDelay field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readmitDelay combined with the force-readmit path (tryReadmit(true)) in BlockNodeSubscriber.getNode(), where inactive nodes are force-readmitted when no active node has the required block, by ensuring all active nodes return EMPTY_BLOCK_RANGE (via timeout manipulation), causing force-readmitted nodes to immediately fail again and be re-deactivated, creating an infinite deactivation loop?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: maxBlockItems field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BlockAssembler's append() method which throws BlockStreamException when pendingCount > maxBlockItems, by observing that this exception is caught in streamBlocks() as a BlockStreamException, calls onError(), and then re-throws, causing the calling code in AbstractBlockSource to propagate the error upward to CompositeBlockSource which increments its own error counter, potentially triggering a source switch that further disrupts block ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: responseTimeout field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the withDeadlineAfter(streamProperties.getResponseTimeout()) call in BlockNode.getBlockRange() by causing the block node's server status response to be delayed by exactly responseTimeout+1ms, making every server status check fail with DEADLINE_EXCEEDED, so that getBlockRange() always returns EMPTY_BLOCK_RANGE and no node is ever selected for streaming?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of validation that maxStreamResponseSize is greater than a minimum meaningful block size, by observing that if maxStreamResponseSize is configured to a value smaller than a typical block, every block response will exceed the limit and trigger a gRPC RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED error, causing all nodes to be deactivated after maxSubscribeAttempts failures?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of exponential backoff in the readmit mechanism (readmitDelay is fixed, not increasing), by causing repeated cycles of maxSubscribeAttempts failures followed by readmitDelay waits, maintaining a predictable attack cadence that keeps nodes perpetually deactivated without any increasing cost to the attacker?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: readmitDelay field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readmitDelay to cause a data gap in the mirror node's block history, where during the readmitDelay window (up to 1 minute) no blocks are ingested, and after recovery the system attempts to catch up but the gap in block numbers causes verifyBlockNumber() to throw InvalidStreamFileException for non-consecutive blocks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: maxBlockItems field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxBlockItems defaults to 800,000 and the minimum is 1,000, by observing that a rogue block node can send a single BlockItemSet containing exactly maxBlockItems items (all valid), followed by one more item in a second BlockItemSet, causing the pendingCount to exceed the limit and triggering BlockStreamException without any malformed data?
